title: soulangia orientalis
parts: Soulangia orientalis has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, flowers, and roots, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The leaves of Soulangia orientalis are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers of the plant contain glycosides and phenolic compounds, which are believed to possess antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The roots of Soulangia orientalis are also a valuable source of medicinal compounds, including saponins and steroids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
side_effects: Soulangia orientalis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems in some individuals. Excessive consumption of the plant's extract has been linked to liver damage and gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged use of soulangia orientalis may also lead to interactions with certain medications, affecting their efficacy or increasing the risk of adverse effects. In rare cases, improper use of the plant has been associated with more severe side effects, including seizures and cardiovascular complications.
